assumptions.travelModel: Historical median speed from CAD data with a 1.18 road detour factor.

algorithms.usedFor: Convert incident coordinates into a demand-intensity surface and generate hotspot candidate sites.
algorithms.whyChosen: Best fit for finding latent fire-demand clusters before any location optimization.

algorithms.usedFor: Choose exactly 3 station locations that maximize weighted incident coverage within the 8-minute SLA.
algorithms.whyChosen: Matches the fixed-budget requirement of placing 3 stations across the map.

algorithms.usedFor: Evaluate how likely incidents are to remain covered when units at a station are already busy.
algorithms.whyChosen: Necessary because your overflow scenario depends on station availability, not just distance.

algorithms.usedFor: Estimate zone-specific availability using different busy probabilities by demand zone.
algorithms.whyChosen: The CSV shows uneven demand and status mix across zones, so one global busy rate would be too coarse.

algorithms.usedFor: Replay concurrent incidents with station capacities, dispatch queues, and overflow responses.
algorithms.whyChosen: This is the clearest way to quantify how SLAs degrade when two incidents occur near each other.

overflowScenario.narrative: Incident A consumes both units at the nearest station, so a second nearby fire must overflow to the next best station.
